terms.definition: A build tool that combines and optimizes many modules/assets into browser-ready output.
Converting code from one form to another, e.g. TypeScript or modern JS to broadly-supported JavaScript.
Static elimination of unused (dead) code from the final bundle, enabled by ES module imports.
Reducing code size by removing whitespace and shortening names without changing behavior.
Breaking output into multiple chunks so parts can be loaded lazily on demand.
A modern build tool offering a fast esbuild-powered dev server and a Rollup-based production build.
Updating modules in a running app during development without a full page reload.
